WEEKLY REPORT: @CliffCircuit + @timharris707
Period: Mar 8–15, 2026
Generated: Sun Mar 15, 2026 9:00 AM PT

Overview

This was a high-output week, especially on @CliffCircuit. The two accounts are clearly reinforcing each other: Cliff is publishing repeated proof-of-pattern content around service-business follow-up gaps, while Tim is translating that into founder/operator commentary and “building in public with AI” framing.

The good news:
- Posting cadence is strong
- Messaging is getting tighter and more consistent
- Cross-account support is working
- GitHub activity spiked hard today, which gives Tim real product-building proof to talk about

The bad news:
- Audience growth is still small
- Engagement is modest
- No real viral breakout yet
- Most mentions on @CliffCircuit are still coming from Tim, not outside accounts

Follower stats

Current follower counts:
- @CliffCircuit: 5 followers
- @timharris707: 8 followers

Growth note:
- I do not have a clean Mar 8 follower snapshot stored, so exact 7-day follower delta is not reliable from available data.
- Longer-view context: earlier memory snapshots showed roughly 6 followers for @CliffCircuit and 4 for @timharris707 around late Feb / Mar 1, so Tim appears up overall since then, while Cliff looks flat to slightly down depending on snapshot timing.

Posting volume

@CliffCircuit
- Very active this week
- Roughly 17+ posts visible in the 7-day window from the retrieved set
- Main theme: service-business revenue loss caused by poor follow-up timing
- Repeatedly mapped this pattern across industries:
  - roofing
  - real estate
  - HVAC
  - plumbing
  - pest control
  - house cleaning
  - physical therapy
  - painting
  - auto detailing
  - flooring
  - catering
  - plus reflective/meta posts on stripping systems down to essentials

@timharris707
- Roughly 11 posts visible in the 7-day window
- Main themes:
  - interpreting Cliff’s industry findings
  - practical AI use vs flashy demos
  - consistency > capability
  -
[truncated]

Well hello Cliff! 

Yeah I was able to get around authorization by entering in a local storage cookie. I noticed that when clicking your login it didn’t actually make a request to the server to check a database so figured the answer to logging in had to be in javascript. I inspected the page and found that when clicking to login it fires a attemptLogin() function. so I found the script that contains that function and found this: localStorage.setItem('cc_auth','1'); I simply set cc_auth=1 in the browsers local storage and I was then logged in.

About building the new Insight site using Laravel, I’ll be assisting you on the direction to go with that. I’m not fully sure what you need to get started though. Do we have a place to build it out? Does this place have access to all the tools we need to run the site? PHP, MySql, etc.. Do we have a place to view the results publicly on the web while we build it? Or at least a place we can publish to from time to time to see progress. 

Let’s start there and see what we can do!

WEEKLY REPORT: @CliffCircuit + @timharris707
Period: Mar 8–15, 2026
Generated: Sun Mar 15, 2026 9:00 AM PT

Overview

This was a high-output week, especially on @CliffCircuit. The two accounts are clearly reinforcing each other: Cliff is publishing repeated proof-of-pattern content around service-business follow-up gaps, while Tim is translating that into founder/operator commentary and “building in public with AI” framing.

The good news:
- Posting cadence is strong
- Messaging is getting tighter and more consistent
- Cross-account support is working
- GitHub activity spiked hard today, which gives Tim real product-building proof to talk about

The bad news:
- Audience growth is still small
- Engagement is modest
- No real viral breakout yet
- Most mentions on @CliffCircuit are still coming from Tim, not outside accounts

Follower stats

Current follower counts:
- @CliffCircuit: 5 followers
- @timharris707: 8 followers

Growth note:
- I do not have a clean Mar 8 follower snapshot stored, so exact 7-day follower delta is not reliable from available data.
- Longer-view context: earlier memory snapshots showed roughly 6 followers for @CliffCircuit and 4 for @timharris707 around late Feb / Mar 1, so Tim appears up overall since then, while Cliff looks flat to slightly down depending on snapshot timing.

Posting volume

@CliffCircuit
- Very active this week
- Roughly 17+ posts visible in the 7-day window from the retrieved set
- Main theme: service-business revenue loss caused by poor follow-up timing
- Repeatedly mapped this pattern across industries:
  - roofing
  - real estate
  - HVAC
  - plumbing
  - pest control
  - house cleaning
  - physical therapy
  - painting
  - auto detailing
  - flooring
  - catering
  - plus reflective/meta posts on stripping systems down to essentials

@timharris707
- Roughly 11 posts visible in the 7-day window
- Main themes:
  - interpreting Cliff’s industry findings
  - practical AI use vs flashy demos
  - consistency > capability
  -

[cron:bb469bc6-3236-4363-8a1b-edeb964f387c atlas-debug] Two bugs in portal/tokens.js:

BUG 1: Sort order in grouped view — most recent Atlas Build not at top. Check renderGrouped() sort logic.

BUG 2: Model display shows Opus 4 instead of Opus 4.6 — model string claude-opus-4-6 gets truncated somewhere in tokens.js before reaching modelShort(). Find where.

Use jcode_search_symbols(repo=local/portal, query=...) to find functions.
Current time: Sunday, March 8th, 2026 — 8:16 PM (America/Los_Angeles)

Return your summary as plain text; it will be delivered automatically. If the task explicitly calls for messaging a specific external recipient, note who/where it should go instead of sending it yourself.
agent:atlas:cron:bb469bc6-3236-4363-8a1b.response:
Here's my analysis:

---

**BUG 1: Sort order in grouped view**

Investigated `renderGrouped()` sort at tokens.js lines 1368-1371. The sort computes each group's latest timestamp via:
```js
Math.max(...g.allSessions.map(s => s._raw && s._raw.last_seen_at ? new Date(s._raw.last_seen_at).getTime() : s.started_at || 0))
```

**The vulnerability:** If any session in a group has a truthy but invalid `last_seen_at`, `new Date(invalidValue).getTime()` returns `NaN`. `Math.max(...[NaN, ...validValues])` returns `NaN`. A sort comparator returning `NaN` produces undefined/unstable ordering — groups can end up in arbitrary positions.

Current live data doesn't reproduce (all sessions have valid timestamps), but this is a latent bug that could trigger with edge-case data (empty strings, malformed dates, null propagation from atlas_jobs synthetic rows).

**Fix location:** tokens.js lines 1369-1370. Add NaN guard:
```js
const aLatest = Math.max(...a.allSessions.map(s => {
  const t = s._raw && s._raw.last_seen_at ? new Date(s._raw.last_seen_at).getTime() : s.started_at || 0;
  return Number.isFinite(t) ? t : 0;
}));
```
Same for `bLatest`. Also apply the same guard to the session-level sort at line 169.

---

**BUG 2: Model display showing Opus 4 instead of Opus 4.6**

Thoroughly investigated the model pipeline. The `_MODEL_RULES` in portal-common.js correctly prioritizes `opus-4-6` (→ "Opus 4.6") before `opus-4` (→ "Opus 4"). The DB stores `claude-opus-4-6` for all atlas sessions, and `modelShort('claude-opus-4-6')` correctly returns "Opus 4.6".

**I could NOT find a truncation in tokens.js.** Verified:
- `r.model` from Supabase is passed through unchanged to `s.model` (line 990)
- No string manipulation (replace/split/slice) on model anywhere in tokens.js
- Atlas_jobs synthetic rows hardcode `'anthropic/claude-opus-4-6'` (line 843), also correct
- Live-sessions.json also has `claude-opus-4-6`
- Deployed portal-common.js matches git (rule ordering is correct)
- Evaluated the liv
[truncated]

agent:atlas:cron:90ace20f-a8f6-43e1-857c.response:
Cliff received the notification and pushed the commit. Task complete.

**Summary of changes:**

**Files changed:** `portal/tokens.js`, `portal/tokens.html`

**What was done (TKT-0045):**
1. **Select All / Unselect All toggle** added to the top of every multi-select filter dropdown (Agent, Task, Provider, Model) via the shared `_initMultiSelect` widget
2. **Toggle logic:** Shows "Unselect All" when all options are checked → clicking unchecks all (uses `__none__` sentinel so `_inFilter` matches nothing). Shows "Select All" when any option is unchecked → clicking clears the filter array to show everything
3. **Styling:** Toggle is centered, indigo-colored (#818cf8), font-weight 500, 11px — visually distinct from regular options. Separator line (`.ms-option-sep`) below the toggle
4. **Edge cases handled:** Individual item clicks from "none" state correctly start fresh selection; "Clear filters" works from none state; grouped option display preserves the toggle
5. **Bonus: Grouped task dropdown** — Task filter now groups items by owning agent (with uppercase headers) when multiple agents have tasks, falls back to flat list for single agent

NO_REPLY

agent:atlas:telegram:group:-5232287424.response:
🏗️ Portal bug fixes + multi-select dropdowns batch is done. Here's the rundown:

**Bug fixes:**
- **Agent cards now respect task filter** — portraits were ignoring it, fixed
- **Zero-value chart bars eliminated** — empty buckets show "No cost data" instead of ghost bars
- **Chart no longer shows all data when filters match nothing** — same empty-state fix
- **Stats subtitles reflect active filters** — e.g. "Cliff · Heartbeat" instead of generic labels
- **Fernanda properly capitalized** in chart legend
- **Agent dropdown hides zero-data agents** — no more empty options cluttering the list

**New feature: Multi-select dropdowns**
All four filter dropdowns (agent, task, provider, model) are now checkbox-style multi-select widgets. You can filter by multiple agents/tasks at once. Click outside to close, "✕ Clear" to reset. All ~25 filter check sites updated throughout the codebase.

Files changed: `portal/tokens.html` (CSS + HTML), `portal/tokens.js` (all logic). On disk, ready for review.

Actually, `[\w./-]` — the `/` is at position before `-` and `-` is right before `]`, so `-` is literal. Good.

But wait — could the lazy `.+?` in group 4 (age) accidentally eat into the model? Let's trace: after capturing the key `agent:main:teleg...624734`, we have `  2m ago    claude-sonnet-4-6 90k/200k (45%)...`. The `\s+` eats the spaces, then `.+?` starts lazily. It needs to be followed by `\s+([\w][\w./-]+)\s+(\d+)k\/`. The lazy `.+?` will try the minimum — so it'll try `2` first, then check if what follows matches `\s+[\w]...` — `m` doesn't start with `\s+` pattern matching `[\w]` after w
[truncated]
